name: Quail Roasted with Vanilla, Coffee and Spices
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons freshly ground espresso coffee (ground very fine)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 300°F.
2. On a baking sheet, arrange the sesame seeds, peppercorns, coriander seeds, cloves, juniper berries, and cinnamon in separate piles.
3. Toast until the sesame seeds are golden colored (about 25 minutes).
4. In a mortar and pestle or hand coffee grinder, finely grind the toasted spices along with the bay leaf.
5. Add the coffee, salt, and sugar; continue grinding until all is well pulverized.
6. Lightly rub the spice mixture over the skin of the quails.
7. Cover and refrigerate for 2 to 3 hours.
8. In a sauté pan, heat oil and cook the quail for about 5 minutes, browning on all sides. If necessary, do in two batches and keep quail warm while completing the cooking.
9. Quail should be cooked medium rare and still juicy and pink.
10. Serve two quails per person in a pool of the vanilla scented butter sauce (recipe follows). Garnish with thinly sliced parsnips that have been quickly deep fried until crisp and sprigs of mint.
